compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: GATE-DESIGN FINDING (from claiming tepid-bus-pomp, discovered with Joe): claiming a CHILD requires the PARENT epic to hold a fresh plan-review attestation (the claim of the child failed with 'Parent error: cannot claim <epic>: attestation is stale'). Two smells: (1) Converting the child to a BUG correctly exempts the bug's OWN plan-review — but the parent-epic coupling re-introduces the very friction the bug-exemption removes; should claiming a bug child require the parent epic's plan-review at all? (2) The epic's attestation is UNSCOPED (no file_impact + no citations → whole-HEAD freshness, the worm-folly-barge residual) so it goes stale on ANY commit, making the parent perpetually un-fresh during active multi-PR epic work. Candidates: exempt bug-children from the parent-review coupling; OR scope epic attestations (container file_impact = union of children?); OR don't require parent plan-review to claim a child (claim the parent's STATUS independently). Fold into the (a)-vs-(b) brainstorm.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
BRAINSTORM DECISION (AC #1) — option (b)/hybrid: COMPLETION-AWARE container plan-review.

Chosen over (a) exempt-epics (throws away the container security review ADR 0016 added) and over a
freshness-axis redesign (rejected: code-drift invalidation is INTENTIONAL — a container's plan is
reviewed against codebase state; e.g. G6 runs at container scope). This epic changes only what a
legitimately re-fired review DOES, not when it fires.

Re-fire triggers are legitimate + infrequent: a paused epic reopened (ADR 0009), or a material
course-correction to the epic plan (material-fingerprint). The claim cascade is NOT a driver — it
only fires while the parent is `open`, so an in_progress epic does not re-gate on further child
claims (`_resolve_open_parent` returns None for a non-open parent).

Design: a deterministic Pass-3 "completion floor" (third floor beside novelty ADR 0008 + drift-
refresh ADR 0002), fed by a SEPARATE `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call (mirrors
`plan_review_novelty`) answering three atomic orthogonal sub-questions per finding —
ATTRIBUTION (which closed child; structural for G3/G4 via `_container_child`), CONTAINMENT (limited
to that child vs spans an open sibling/system), LAYER (plan-semantics vs delivered-functionality).
Drop iff ALL: attribution=DELIVERED-NOW child (valid completion attestation via validity-on-read,
OR superseded by an in-epic sibling) AND containment=limited AND layer=plan-semantics AND
priority<floor AND criterion NOT in {security,contract}. Every ambiguous answer
(insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality) fails toward KEEP; drops go to the
REVIEW_RESULT sidecar and are recomputed each run.

KEY EDGE DECISIONS: in-progress children stay FULLY in scope (v1); force-close is NEVER trusted as
delivered (closes the force-close laundering hole); abandoned children stay in scope (a real plan
hole to surface); superseded-by-in-epic-sibling IS treated as delivered-elsewhere (prevents the
stale-closed-ticket hard-block Joe has observed). DEFERRED (separate epics): partial-completion
attestation for in-progress children; a structured `resolution` enum on `close`; the sidecar
re-injection queue; an optional second LAYER binary (calibration-gated).

Grounding: Tricorder/SonarQube/GitHub code-scanning (suppress on delivered+below-severity+
non-actionable, severity/security hard veto, never attribution-alone, never deletion); G-Eval/
CheckList/FLASK (few frozen atomic binaries + deterministic aggregation); ODC + IEEE-1028 (once
delivered, governing review shifts plan-review -> code-review; plan-semantics feedback goes moot,
delivered-substance stays live). Full trail in the session log.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "epic plan-review is certified PASS (blocking=0, manifest signed); claim blocked ONLY by stale-head — the unscoped-container attestation stamped head_sha d4a0a2a4 (attested-mode ref) vs working HEAD 49ad69b, a ref-resolution artifact of the very unscoped-freshness limitation THIS epic fixes, not a content failure. Proceeding under user directive to fully implement the epic.".

compiled_state.description:
## Context

When the plan-review claim gate re-fires on a CONTAINER ticket (epic/story with children) that is
mid-execution, it reviews the plan as if FRESH — blind to which children are already delivered. It
then re-litigates acceptance criteria already shipped by closed+attested children: false BLOCKs and
coaching to tighten AC that is done, plus a slow whole-context review of settled work.

Re-fires are legitimate but infrequent — a paused epic reopened (ADR 0009 invalidates an
attestation signed before the reopen), or a material course-correction to the epic plan
(material-fingerprint invalidation). The claim CASCADE is not the driver (it only fires while the
parent is `open`). Code-drift invalidation is INTENTIONAL and unchanged (a container's plan is
reviewed against codebase state — G6 runs at container scope): this epic changes only what a
re-fired review DOES, not when it fires.

## Success Criteria

- A re-fired container review does not re-litigate delivered AC: findings limited to a
  delivered-now child's plan-semantics are dropped; delivered-functionality, cross-sibling, and
  severe/security/contract findings are always preserved.
- Suppression is deterministic in Pass-3, keyed on VERIFIED delivery (completion attestation or
  in-epic supersede), never on bare `closed` status; force-closed/abandoned children stay in scope.
- No change to WHEN a review is invalidated (code-drift stays), no `close`-op change, no new
  attestation kind.

## What we build

A deterministic Pass-3 "completion floor" (third floor beside the novelty floor ADR 0008 and the
drift-refresh path ADR 0002), fed by a separate `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call (mirroring
`plan_review_novelty`) that answers three atomic orthogonal sub-questions per finding: ATTRIBUTION
(which closed child), CONTAINMENT (limited vs spans open), LAYER (plan-semantics vs
delivered-functionality). Pass-1 stays completion-blind (full recall); the coach is unchanged.

## Scope

- IN: the Pass-2 completion sub-call + three sub-questions; the Pass-3 completion floor + config
  evidence-gate + priority floor; the delivered-now predicate (attestation reuse + in-epic sibling
  supersede); sidecar recording; calibration gold-set + tests.
- OUT (deferred, separate epics): partial-completion attestation for in-progress children; a
  structured `resolution` enum on `close`; the sidecar re-injection queue; an optional second LAYER
  binary. In-progress children stay FULLY in scope in v1.

## Alternatives considered

- (a) Exempt epics from the claim gate — REJECTED: discards the container security review ADR 0016
  added (a container epic that stands up infrastructure would go unreviewed).
- (b) Completion-aware container review — CHOSEN; hybrid = (b) + keying on VERIFIED delivery
  (completion attestation) + in-epic sibling supersede.
- Decomposition-drift-only freshness (never invalidate a container on code drift) — REJECTED:
  code-drift invalidation is intentional; a container's plan IS reviewed vs codebase state (G6 runs
  at container scope).
- A single composite predicate instead of three sub-questions — REJECTED: attribution / containment
  / layer are orthogonal (identity / spatial scope / artifact layer); folding them loses the
  distinctions and re-couples model category + severity error.
- Hide delivered children / review only open children — REJECTED: blinds coherence + security to
  delivered<->open interactions; we FREEZE delivered children as visible context, not hide them.
- Tag- or progress-note-based suppression — REJECTED: unsigned + gameable (prompt-injection surface
  into a security-sensitive gate); we key on the signed completion attestation.
- Run the completion-verifier to drive scoping — REJECTED: FAIL-by-construction mid-flight + signing
  ambiguity (needs a third attestation kind); we READ the attestation closed children already carry.
- Reuse the novelty sub-answer mechanism (add the three completion questions to NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS)
  instead of a parallel sub-call — REJECTED: completion keys on a DIFFERENT context input (the
  delivered-children manifest) than novelty (prior findings), so a separate sub-call preserves the
  Pass-1 independence invariant by construction; they share the kernel sub-answer machinery, not context.

## Acceptance Criteria

Decision (recorded, not a forward deliverable): option (b)/hybrid chosen — see the decision comment, '## Alternatives considered', and the completion session log.
- [ ] A separate `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call (mirroring `plan_review_novelty`) emits, per finding, {attribution, containment, layer} with fail-safe values (insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality all keep).
- [ ] Pass-3 completion floor drops a finding iff attribution=delivered-now-child AND containment=limited AND layer=plan-semantics AND priority<floor AND criterion not in {security,contract}; gated behind an evidence-gate flag + configurable priority floor (mirroring the novelty floor).
- [ ] `delivered-now` = valid completion attestation (validity-on-read, reusing the `_child_closure_findings` predicate) OR superseded by a sibling under the same parent; force-closed/abandoned/in-progress children are NOT delivered and stay in scope.
- [ ] Drops recorded to the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (joinable, observable) and recomputed every run (a reopened/un-delivered child auto-resurfaces its findings).
- [ ] A durable ADR (`docs/adr/0020-completion-aware-container-plan-review.md`) codifies the new contracts (sub-call schema + closed enums, delivered-now predicate, completion floor + config, sidecar drop_reason) — inline AC + session log are not the durable record.
- [ ] Tests: a partially-complete epic review drops delivered plan-semantics findings yet preserves (a) a delivered child's delivered-functionality/security finding, (b) a finding spanning an open sibling, (c) all findings on a force-closed child; plus a calibration gold-set of must-never-suppress cases.
compiled_state.title: Completion-aware plan-review for container tickets (epics/stories with children)
